title: LLM attention-tail stress ladder
hypothesis: A bounded larger-sequence attention-tail stress campaign can determine whether the previous low softmax occupancy and zero wait/backpressure evidence was caused by insufficient benchmark pressure or by a true lack of softmax scheduler bottleneck.

direct_comparison.primary_question: Do larger repeated attention-tail proxy models expose softmax occupancy, dependency waits, or backpressure that justify a focused architecture proposal?
include: 4-6 non-terminal Softmax episodes per model path
existing fp16_nm1 and fp16_nm2 architecture points
per-token latency, throughput, softmax occupancy, wait, and backpressure evidence
exclude: new softmax datapath changes in this proposal

expected_benefit: checks whether attention-tail pressure was previously too small before launching datapath work
keeps softmax buffering, pipeline partitioning, and scheduler-overlap proposals evidence-gated
records the next benchmark-ladder step between attention-tail smoke evidence and practical decoder-style workloads
risks: the stress suite is still an ONNX-lite proxy and may not expose KV-cache or decoder-quality bottlenecks
larger shapes may increase runtime without changing the current bottleneck classification
